task.title: Report invalid stored payload versions explicitly
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/374 by classifying persisted payload-version integers outside the public u32 domain as explicit replay data errors rather than generic SQLite failures.
acceptance_criteria: Replaying a row whose payload_version is 4294967296 returns a dedicated error carrying that exact value.
The invalid stored payload-version error has no underlying error source.
Supported payload versions and decoder behavior remain unchanged.
ReplayError remains extensible for downstream callers and the public documentation describes the stable contract.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused integration test that persists a payload version one greater than u32::MAX and requires an explicit replay diagnostic.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test received event-log storage error: Integer 4294967296 out of range at index 2, proving replay misclassified a representational data error as failed SQLite access.

attempts.summary: Moved payload-version conversion to an explicit replay boundary.
attempts.patch: Read payload_version as SQLite's native i64, converted it with u32::try_from, and mapped conversion failure to ReplayError::InvalidStoredPayloadVersion while preserving the original i64 value.

attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit correctly identified that adding a variant to exhaustive ReplayError could break downstream exhaustive matches.
attempts.patch: Marked ReplayError non-exhaustive and updated the error-contract documentation to require downstream fallback arms for both public event-log error enums.

attempts.summary: Reran local verification and monitored both required pull-request checks after the review fix.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit and Rust quality gate run 29303956889 passed on the corrected commit; pull request 375 squash-merged as ab6307c.
attempts.patch: No further corrective patch was required.

outcome.summary: Replay now distinguishes a valid SQLite integer that cannot be represented by the Event API from an actual storage failure, preserves the offending value, and keeps its public error surface explicitly extensible.

verification.command: GitHub Rust quality gate run 29303956889 for pull request 375
CodeRabbit review for pull request 375 # compatibility finding fixed in cc0c5b8 and the final status passed
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0013-stored-payload-version.json # result: valid development record
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 13 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Read SQLite INTEGER values in their native signed domain before converting to a narrower public type so conversion failures retain the authoritative stored value.
A successful query can still reveal incompatible persisted data; classify that separately from storage access failure.
Make public error enums non-exhaustive before extending them so downstream wildcard matches remain source-compatible.
A focused boundary-value test at u32::MAX plus one makes the representational mismatch deterministic and documents the exact contract.
